The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, introduces significant changes to the clean energy tax credit landscape established by the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA). The OBBBA enhances domestic content requirements and makes them mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y) for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025. Specifically, the law harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y, introducing stricter rules for projects starting after June 16, 2025. This information comes from credible sources, including Arnold & Porter and White & Case LLP, which provide detailed analyses of the new law and its implications for clean energy tax credits.

The Senate Finance Committee's reconciliation bill also updates domestic content percentages for Section 48E projects, further supporting the notion that domestic content compliance will become mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility in 2025.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X).
